commands.name: generate-contents
commands.description:
Generates content items and the corresponding schema.
Use "Squidex.CLI.dll ai [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --schemaName
options.description:
<TEXT>
The optional schema name to override the result.

options.name: --key
options.description:
<TEXT>
The api key.

options.name: --no-schema
options.description: Do not create the schema.

options.name: --no-contents
options.description: Do not create the schema.

options.name: --images
options.description: Indicates if images should be generated.

options.name: --delete-schema
options.description: Delete the previous schema, if it exists.

options.name: --model
options.description:
<TEXT>    [gpt-4-turbo]
The OpenAI chat model

options.name: --image-model
options.description:
<TEXT>    [dall-e-2]
The OpenAI image model

options.name: --num-contents
options.description:
<NUMBER>  [3]
The number of content items to create.

options.name: --num-attempts
options.description:
<NUMBER>  [3]
The number of attempts. If an error happens the CLI will try to fix it with the AI.

options.name: --language
options.description:
<TEXT>    [en]
The supported languages.

options.name: --execute
options.description:
Execture the result and do not describe it.
Use descriptions with the following syntax:

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --confirm
options.description:
<TEXT>
Confirm the name of the app.

commands.name: create
commands.description:
Create and download an backup.
Use "Squidex.CLI.dll backup [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --timeout
options.description:
<NUMBER>  [30]
The timeout to wait for the backup in minutes.

options.name: --interval
options.description:
<NUMBER>  [30]
The query interval to test if the backup is ready in seconds.

options.name: --deleteAfterDownload
options.description: Defines if the created backup shall be deleted from app after the backup task is completed.

options.name: --force
options.description: Overwrites the file if it already exists.

options.name: --url
options.description:
<TEXT>
The optional url to your squidex installation. Default: https://cloud.squidex.io.

options.name: --label
options.description:
<TEXT>
Optional label for this app.

options.name: --create
options.description: Create the app if it does not exist (needs admin client).

options.name: --ignore-self-signed
options.description: Ignores self signed certificates.

options.name: --use
options.description: Use the config.

options.name: --header
options.description:
<TEXT>
Adds a custom header in the format (Key=Value)

options.name: --table
options.description: Output as table.

commands.name: use
commands.description:
Use an app.
Use "Squidex.CLI.dll config [command] --help" for more information about a command.

commands.name: enrich-defaults
commands.description: Enrich the content items with its defaults.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --unpublished
options.description: Handle unpublished content.

options.name: --required-fields
options.description: Also enrich required fields.

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export the content for a schema.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --filter
options.description:
<TEXT>
Optional filter.

options.name: --text
options.description:
<TEXT>
Optional full text query.

options.name: --order
options.description:
<TEXT>
Optional ordering.

options.name: --output
options.description:
<TEXT>
Optional file or folder name. Default: Schema name.

options.name: --delimiter
options.description:
<TEXT>            [;]
The csv delimiter.

options.name: --unpublished
options.description: Export unpublished content.

options.name: --multiple
options.description: Creates one file per content.

options.name: --fields
options.description:
<TEXT>
Comma separated list of fields. CSV only.

options.name: --full
options.description: Write full entities, not only data when exporting as CSV. Default: false.

options.name: --format
options.description:
<IMEXPORTFORMAT>
Defines the output format.
Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated
Use the following format to define fields for CSV:

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --unpublished
options.description: Import unpublished content.

options.name: --count
options.description:
<NUMBER>  [10]
The number of items.

options.name: --file
options.description:
<TEXT>
The optional path to the file.

commands.name: import
commands.description:
Import the content to a schema.
Use "Squidex.CLI.dll contents [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --unpublished
options.description: Import unpublished content.

options.name: --fields
options.description:
<TEXT>
Comma separated list of fields to import.

options.name: --delimiter
options.description:
<TEXT>            [;]
The csv delimiter.

options.name: --key
options.description:
<TEXT>
The key field to use.

options.name: --format
options.description:
<IMEXPORTFORMAT>
Defines the input format.
Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated
Use the following format to define fields from the CSV/JSON file:

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --print
options.description: Only print the request to see the fields to be created.

arguments.name: THE FIELDS IN THE FOLLOWING FORMAT 'FIELD1=ASC,FIELD2=DESC'

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

commands.name: sync
commands.description:
Sync the schema.
Use "Squidex.CLI.dll schemas [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --schema
options.description:
<TEXT>
The new schema name.

options.name: --no-delete
options.description: Do not delete fields.

options.name: --no-recreate
options.description: Do not recreate fields.

options.name: --no-ref-fix
options.description: Do not fix referenced.

arguments.name: THE FILE WITH THE SCHEMA JSON.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

commands.name: in
commands.description: Imports the app from a folder.

options.name: --app
options.description:
<TEXT>
The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

options.name: --targets
options.description:
<TEXT>
The targets to sync, e.g. 'sync in -t contents -t schemas'. Use 'sync targets' to view all targets.

options.name: --language
options.description:
<TEXT>
The content language to synchronize.

options.name: --content-action
options.description:
<CONTENTACTION>
Defines how to handle content.
Allowed values: Upsert, UpsertPatch, Create, Update, Patch

options.name: --delete
options.description: Use this flag to also delete entities.

options.name: --patch-content
options.description: Make content updates as patch.

options.name: --recreate
options.description: Use this flag to also recreate entities.

options.name: --skip-assets
options.description: Use this flag to sync asset folders but not assets.

options.name: --update-current-client
options.description: Also update the client that is used during the sync process.

options.name: --emulate
options.description: Use this flag to not make any updates and to emulate the changes.

commands.name: new
commands.description: Creates a new folder with sample files how to create an app from json files.

options.description:
<TEXT>

options.name: --targets

<TEXT>

options.name: --stream-contents
options.description: Use the new streaming API for contents.

options.name: --max-age
options.description:
<TIMESPAN>
Content & assets created or last modified within time span defined.

options.name: --describe
options.description: Create a README.md file.
